docs.title: Controllers
docs.text: Currently the only supported controllers are Xbox controllers.

docs.title: Prerequisite
docs.text:
• WPILib installation (Build tools, VS Code, etc.)
  • Python installation with MkDocs 1

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: By default running ./gradlew build or ./gradlew publish will generate a vendordep that points to the local build directory. using the -PreleaseMode will configure the vendordep to point to the actual hosted maven and json URLs.

docs.title: Useful commands
docs.text:
Main build command ./gradlew build ./gradlew build -PreleaseMode

Publish will generate meven repository and vendordep. ./gradlew publish ./gradlew publish -PreleaseMode

This was useful once for something, but we don't remember. ./gradlew installRoboRIOToolchain

Clean command ./gradlew clean

Generates gradle wrapper ./gradlew wrapper

Spotless is run automatically, but if you want to manually trigger it run this ./gradlew spotlessApply

Get the vendordep command to import loggerhead into a robot project. Useful for development. ./gradlew getDevVendordep

docs.title: Docs
docs.text: Main documentation (not javadoc) is built using MkDocs. Note there are currently no scripts to automatically setup a venv with mkdocs because we can't be bothered to fight with gradle for the time being.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. cd <project-root>/docs
  2. mkdocs <build|serve> (You might need to use python -m mkdocs ... if you are not using a venv)

docs.title: Javadoc
docs.text:
Javadocs are built when you use ./gradlew build and publish

  1. Python is only necessery for building and editing documentation. ↩
